
```{r eval=FALSE}
library(devtools)
library(testthat)

devtools::document()
devtools::test()
devtools::check()
```

## National CDC BRFSS

```{r}
library(brfssTools)

# 1. Run the audit and save it (so you don't have to re-run)
vintage <- c(2012:2024)
brfss_download(vintage)
cw_nat <- brfss_crosswalk(dataset = "National", year = vintage) 
audit_nat <- brfss_crosswalk_audit(cw_nat)
saveRDS(audit_nat, "~/audit_results_nat.rds")
audit_nat <- rio::import("~/audit_results_nat.rds")

# 2. See how big the problem is
audit_nat |> dplyr::count(needs_action)

# 3. Look at the unique raw_var_names that fail. Group similar ones.
audit_nat |>
  dplyr::filter(needs_action) |>
  dplyr::count(raw_var_name, sort = TRUE)

audit_nat |>
  dplyr::filter(needs_action) |>
  dplyr::count(raw_var_name, sort = TRUE) |>
  print(n = 50)

# 4. Decide retag rules. Most are "yes, retag to OR." A few might be
#    "no, leave alone — that's a National variable that got renamed in
#    that year." A few might be "delete, this row was a mistake."

# 5. Edit concept_map_brfss.csv to reflect those decisions. This can be:
#    - a single dplyr pipeline that overwrites source for matching rows
#    - or hand-editing the CSV in Excel for a few hundred rows
#    - or whatever feels right

# 6. Save the file back
# readr::write_csv(cm_updated,
#                 "inst/extdata/concept_map_brfss.csv", na = "")

# 7. Rebuild package, run tests, commit. Done.
```

## BYOD Oregon

```{r}
brfss_set_pool("OR", "//nas3/Hlth-Home/nathanp9/data-repo/oha__behavioral_risk_factor_surveillance_system/restricted")
cw_or <- brfss_crosswalk(dataset = "OR", year = vintage)
audit_or <- brfss_crosswalk_audit(cw_or)
saveRDS(audit_or, "~/audit_results_or.rds")
audit_or <- rio::import("~/audit_results_or.rds")

# 2. See how big the problem is
audit_or |> dplyr::count(needs_action)

# 3. Look at the unique raw_var_names that fail. Group similar ones.
audit_or |>
  dplyr::filter(needs_action) |>
  dplyr::count(raw_var_name, sort = TRUE)

audit_or |>
  dplyr::filter(needs_action) |>
  dplyr::count(raw_var_name, sort = TRUE) |>
  print(n = 50)
```

```{r}
# Inventory whatever OR pool you have registered
or_inv <- brfss_inventory_pool("OR")

# Spot-check whether OR's files have BMI5 vs _BMI5, STATE vs _STATE
or_inv |>
  dplyr::filter(raw_var_name %in% c(
    "BMI5", "_BMI5",
    "BMI5CAT", "_BMI5CAT",
    "RFBMI5", "_RFBMI5",
    "STATE", "_STATE",
    "AGE", "_AGE_G", "_AGE80"
  )) |>
  dplyr::count(raw_var_name, sort = TRUE)
```

```{r}
or_inv |>
  dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(toupper(raw_var_name), "STATE")) |>
  dplyr::count(raw_var_name, sort = TRUE)
```

```{r}
cm <- readr::read_csv(
  system.file("extdata", "concept_map_brfss.csv", package = "brfssTools")
)
cm |>
  dplyr::filter(raw_var_name == "AGE") |>
  dplyr::select(concept_id, source, year, raw_var_name) |>
  dplyr::arrange(year)
```

```{r}
library(brfssTools)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Re-run audit (or load the saved one)
cw    <- brfss_crosswalk(dataset = "National", year = 2012:2024)
audit <- brfss_crosswalk_audit(cw)

# OR inventory (which raw vars exist in OR at which years)
or_inv <- brfss_inventory_pool("OR") |>
  distinct(raw_var_name, year) |>
  mutate(in_or = TRUE)

# Cross-reference: for each needs_action row, is the var in OR at that year?
retag_plan <- audit |>
  filter(needs_action) |>
  left_join(or_inv, by = c("raw_var_name", "year")) |>
  mutate(in_or = coalesce(in_or, FALSE))

# How does the plan break down?
retag_plan |> count(in_or)
# in_or = TRUE  → will retag to OR
# in_or = FALSE → "phantom" rows, kept as core, surfaced for review

# Look at the phantoms
retag_plan |>
  filter(!in_or) |>
  count(raw_var_name, sort = TRUE) |>
  print(n = 30)

```

```{r eval=FALSE}
source("inst/migrate/05-retag-core-to-or.R")

# Dry run first — no files change
migrate_05_retag_core_to_or()
```
